title: jennyella lowiana
intro: Jennyella lowiana, also known as the "Australian desert pea", is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Jennyella lowiana are attributed to its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Jennyella lowiana can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or by drying them to make a powder for later use.
